task_statement: P7c of BRICK 6-surface audit repair (product route / Easy Building surface) - bounded follow-up: resolve or explicitly classify the P7b status/build evidence-root drift, preserve the P7b public route/doc/error-surface improvements, and rerun the P7 slice proof through the official brick build graph route. Do not broaden into full Easy-Building dynamic ergonomics, P8 ship safety, or P9 customer replay.
selection_rule: manual graph because P7c is a focused reroute follow-up from P7b closure concern transition-concern:brick-6-p7-product-route-surface-0701b-status-build-root-drift. Use design -> work -> fan([code-qa, axis-qa, evidence-qa]) -> closure; do not repeat P7a linear pseudo-graph.

nodes.work_statement:
Design the P7c bounded follow-up from P7b closure evidence. Read only the P7b pause doc, P7b graph/support evidence needed for the root-drift concern, and the P7 phase/source audit refs. Produce exact repair scope and checker-first proof plan for status/build evidence-root alignment. Return design only; no source edits.

STRICT SCOPE: phase:P7c product-route surface follow-up ONLY. Primary gap: status/no-args CLI and build output must agree on how the default evidence/build root is explained to customers, or the difference must be explicitly classified as support/internal vs customer-facing with product-safe wording. Preserve P7b slice outcomes: public route names brick init/status/doctor/build/build --graph; no onboard-module seam as customer route; quickstart separates compact graph packet from internal full Building Plan/run_building_plan; CLI plain error line uses product-safe taxonomy, not raw str(exc)/traceback; bare brick default is status. Add or verify negative pins for onboard-module-as-route drift, raw str(exc)/traceback operator leak, and --large/new-engine revival. Do NOT revive --large, do NOT add a new engine, do NOT build full Easy-Building dynamic design ergonomics (S6-F4 remains later/not_proven), do NOT broaden into P8 ship safety or P9 dynamic proof, and do NOT push. Use official build()/brick build language only. Movement vocabulary is forward/reroute; HOLD/pause is lifecycle. QA lanes may use probe_write fixtures only and must not mutate customer source truth. Checker/profile green is support evidence only.

Implement the P7c bounded follow-up. Required outcomes: (1) reconcile or product-safely classify default evidence/build root wording across bare `brick`/`brick status` and `brick build`; (2) preserve existing P7 public route/doc/error-surface improvements; (3) add or verify regression pins for onboard-module route drift, raw operator error leakage, and --large/new-engine revival; (4) run focused brick_cli_entrypoint profile, py_compile for changed Python, git diff --check, and check_profile.py --all, or return exact failure evidence without closing; (5) record full Easy-Building dynamic ergonomics, P8 ship safety, P9 dynamic/customer proof, customer comprehension, and real-provider/fresh-machine claims as not_proven unless separately proven.
